zh.lifeSummary: 當Thomas White於大約1737年出生在美國紐約州納斯奧普斯亞德時，他的父親Thomas White39歲，母親Hannah Curtice52歲。他於1761年2月1日與Dorothea Brass在美國紐約州納斯奧普斯亞德結婚。他們至少是5個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於大約1816年6月21日在加拿大新布隆士威省約克昆斯波利享年80歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Thomas White于大约1737年出生在美国纽约州納斯奧普斯亞德时，他的父亲Thomas White39岁，母亲Hannah Curtice52岁。他于1761年2月1日与Dorothea Brass在美国纽约州納斯奧普斯亞德结婚。他们至少是5个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于大约1816年6月21日在加拿大新布隆士威省約克昆斯波利享年80岁。

nameMeanings.name: White
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: from Middle English white, wit (Old English hwīt ‘white’), hence a nickname for someone with white hair or a pale complexion. In some cases it is perhaps from a Middle English survival of the Old English personal name Hwīta, a short form of names in Hwīt- (from hwīt ‘white’). The name may also be topographic, referring to someone who lived by a bend or curve in a river or road (from Old English wiht ‘bend’), the source of the placename of Great Whyte in Ramsey, Huntingdonshire (compare Wight ). This name is also a variant of Wight . The surname White is also very common among African Americans.

Irish and Scottish: adopted for any of several Irish and Scottish Gaelic names based on bán ‘white, fair’ (see Bain 1, McElwain ) or fionn ‘fair’ (see Finn 1). The English surname has been Gaelicized in Ireland as de Faoite.

Americanized form (translation into English) of various European surnames meaning ‘white’, for example German Weiss , French Blanc , Polish Białas (see Bialas ), Slovenian Belec , or any other synonymous Slavic surname beginning with Bel-, Bev-, Biel- or Bil-.

variants.name: Whitty
meanings: topographic name denoting a ‘dweller by the white hedge or the white (forest) enclosure’, from Middle English whit ‘white’ (Old English hwīt) + heie ‘hedge, enclosure’ (Old English (ge)hæg and Old French haie) perhaps in reference to whitethorn (hawthorn) hedging. This English name has been recorded in Ireland (Wexford) since the 13th century. It has been Gaelicized as de Faoite, see also White .
nickname from Middle English whit ‘white’ + eie ‘eye’ (Old English hwīt + ēage). Compare Blackie 2 and Greeney .
variant of   Witty  , with which this surname may have been confused.

variants.name: Whitson
meanings: English and Scottish (Midlothian): from an unrecorded Middle English personal name Whiteson, literally ‘white son’ or perhaps ‘son of someone named White ’. Occasionally, a variant of Whiston .

variants.name: Whitt
meanings: English (Nottinghamshire): variant of   White  .
Possibly also an Americanized form of German   Witt  .

variants.name: McQuitty
meanings: Irish (northern): Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Fhaoitigh, a patronymic from a Gaelicization of English White . See also McWhite .

variants.name: Whitecotton
meanings: Apparently a compound of   White   +   Cotton  , but the history is obscure.

variants.name: McElwain
meanings: Scottish and Irish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Giolla Bháin (Irish) or Mac Gille Bháin (Scottish) ‘son of the fair-haired lad’, often translated into English as White or Whyte but otherwise indistinguishable, once Anglicized, from Scottish Gaelic Mac Gille Bheathain in 2 below. Compare Kilbane .
Scottish: Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Gille Bheathain ‘son of the servant (i.e. devotee) of (Saint) Beathan’, a personal name representing a diminutive of beatha ‘life’. Compare McBain .

variants.name: McWhite
meanings: Irish: partly translated Anglicized form of Gaelic Mac Faoitigh, a patronymic from a Gaelic form of English White .

variants.name: Wight
meanings: Scottish and English: nickname from Middle English wiht, wight ‘agile, nimble, strong, brave’ (Old Norse víg with the Old Norse neuter suffix -t, for which compare the word scant), or from the corresponding Older Scots word wicht. Possibly sometimes a nickname from Middle English wight ‘living creature; small portion or thing’, or from the corresponding Older Scots word wicht.

English: habitational name from the Isle of Wight. The island is recorded as Vectis (its Latin name) c. 150 AD . Its name derives from a British word, perhaps related to Welsh gwaith ‘turn, course’, meaning ‘place of the division’, in reference to the island's position in the Solent.
English and Scottish: variant of   White  .

variants.name: Whites
meanings: English: variant of White with genitive or post-medieval excrescent -s.

variants.name: Whitman
meanings: English (southern): from the Middle English personal name Whytman (Old English Hwītmann, from hwīt ‘white’ + mann ‘man’). Compare White 1 and also Wightman , with which this surname was probably confused.
History: John Whitman settled in Weymouth, MA, c. 1638. The poet Walt Whitman (1819–92) was descended from Joseph Whitman, who had settled in Stratford, CT, from England around 1660.

nameMeanings.name: Thomas
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: New Testament name, borne by one of Christ's twelve apostles, referred to as ‘Thomas, called Didymus’ (John 11:16; 20:24). Didymos is the Greek word for ‘twin’, and the name is the Greek form of an Aramaic byname meaning ‘twin’. The given name has always been popular throughout Christendom, in part because St Thomas's doubts have made him seem a very human character.

historicEvents.description: Thomas Jefferson's American Declaration of Independence endorsed by Congress. Colonies declare independence.

historicEvents.title: The First Constitution
historicEvents.description: Serving the newly created United States of America as the first constitution, the Articles of Confederation were an agreement among the 13 original states preserving the independence and sovereignty of the states. But with a limited central government, the Constitutional Convention came together to replace the Articles of Confederation with a more established Constitution and central government on where the states can be represented and voice their concerns and comments to build up the nation.

historicEvents.title: Albany is Named Capital of New York
historicEvents.description: Albany became the capital of New York in 1797. Albany is the oldest continuous settlement of the original 13 colonies.

historicEvents.title: Movement to Washington D.C.
historicEvents.description: While the growth of the new nation was exponential, the United States didn’t have permanent location to house the Government. The First capital was temporary in New York City but by the second term of George Washington the Capital moved to Philadelphia for the following 10 years. Ultimately during the Presidency of John Adams, the Capital found a permanent home in the District of Columbia.
France sells Louisiana territories to U.S.A.

en.lifeSummary: When Thomas White was born about 1737, in Hempstead, Nassau, New York, United States, his father, Thomas White, was 39 and his mother, Hannah Curtice, was 52. He married Dorothea Brass on 1 February 1761, in Hempstead, Nassau, New York, United States. They were the parents of at least 5 sons and 1 daughter. He died about 21 June 1816, in Queensbury, York, New Brunswick, Canada, at the age of 80.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Thomas White est né environ 1737, à Hempstead, Nassau, New York, États-Unis, son père, Thomas White, avait 39 ans et sa mère, Hannah Curtice, avait 52 ans. Il a épousé Dorothea Brass le 1 février 1761, à Hempstead, Nassau, New York, États-Unis. Ils ont eu au moins 5 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé environ 21 juin 1816, à Queensbury, York, Nouveau-Brunswick, Canada, à l’âge de 80 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Thomas White um 1737 in Hempstead, Nassau, New York, Vereinigte Staaten geboren wurde, war sein Vater Thomas White 39 und seine Mutter Hannah Curtice 52. Er hat am 1. Februar 1761 in Hempstead, Nassau, New York, Vereinigte Staaten Dorothea Brass geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 5 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Er starb um 21. Juni 1816 im Alter von 80 Jahren in Queensbury, York, New Brunswick, Kanada.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas White è nato il circa 1737 nel Hempstead, Nassau, New York, Stati Uniti d'America, il suo padre, Thomas White, aveva 39 anni e il suo madre, Hannah Curtice, aveva 52 anni. Lui sposato Dorothea Brass il 1 febbraio 1761, nel Hempstead, Nassau, New York, Stati Uniti d'America. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figli e 1 figlia. Morì circa 21 giugno 1816, nel Queensbury, York, Nuovo Brunswick, Canada, all'età di 80 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Thomas Whiteが約1737年にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州ナッサウヘンプステアドで生まれた時，父Thomas Whiteは39歳，母Hannah Curticeは52歳であった。1761年2月1日にアメリカ合衆国ニューヨーク州ナッサウヘンプステアドでDorothea Brassと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は約1816年6月21日にカナダニューブランズウィック州ヨーククイーンスブリーで80歳で死去した。

ko.Shelter Island, Suffolk, New York Colony: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 요르크 콜론이 수폴크 셸터 이슬랜드
ko.Shelter Island, Suffolk, New York, Briti: 미국 뉴욕 주 수폴크 셸터 이슬랜드 셸터 이슬랜드

ko.New York Colony, British Colonial Americ: 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 뉴 요르크 콜론이
ko.lifeSummary: Thomas White(이)가 대략 1737년 미국 뉴욕 주 나소 헤프스테아드에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Thomas White(은)는 39세였고, 그의 어머니 Hannah Curtice(은)는 52세였다. 그는 1761년 2월 1일 미국 뉴욕 주 나소 헤프스테아드에서 Dorothea Brass(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 대략 1816년 6월 21일 캐나다 뉴브런즈윅 요르크 퀸스버이에서 80세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Thomas White nasceu aproximadamente 1737, em Hempstead, Nassau, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América, seu pai, Thomas White, tinha 39 anos e sua mãe, Hannah Curtice, tinha 52 anos. Ele casou-se com Dorothea Brass em 1 de fevereiro de 1761, em Hempstead, Nassau, Nova Iorque, Estados Unidos da América. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhos e 1 filha. Ele faleceu aproximadamente 21 de junho de 1816, em Queensbury, York, Novo Brunswick, Canadá, com 80 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Thomas White родился около 1737 г. в местности хемпстид, нассау, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты, его отцу по имени Thomas White было 39 лет, а матери, которую звали Hannah Curtice, 52. Он женился на женщине по имени Dorothea Brass 1 февраля 1761 г. в местности хемпстид, нассау, Нью-Йорк, Соединенные штаты. У них было по крайней мере 5 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер около 21 июня 1816 г. в местности куинсбури, ерк, Нью-Брансуик, Канада в возрасте 80 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Thomas White nació aproximadamente 1737, en Hempstead, Nassau, Nueva York, Estados Unidos, su papá, Thomas White, tenía 39 años y su mamá, Hannah Curtice, tenía 52 años. Se casó con Dorothea Brass el 1 de febrero de 1761, en Hempstead, Nassau, Nueva York, Estados Unidos. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijos y 1 hija. Murió aproximadamente 21 de junio de 1816, en Queensbury, York, Nuevo Brunswick, Canadá, a la edad de 80 años.
